name: Crop Residue Management (CRM) Scheme — Haryana
description: The Government of Haryana provides financial assistance to farmers and cooperative societies for purchase of crop residue management machinery. Under this scheme, individual farmers receive 50% subsidy and cooperative societies/FPOs/Panchayats receive 80% subsidy on the cost of machinery such as Happy Seeder, Super SMS, Paddy Straw Chopper, etc.
eligibility: Individual farmers with land ownership records
Cooperative societies and FPOs registered in Haryana
Priority given to small and marginal farmers (less than 2 hectares)
howToApply: Visit the official Haryana Agriculture Department portal (agriharyana.gov.in)
Register on the portal using Aadhaar and mobile number
Select the CRM scheme and choose the desired machinery
Upload required documents
Submit the application online
Verification will be done by Block Agriculture Officer
On approval, purchase machinery from an empanelled dealer and claim subsidy
maxAmount: ₹80,000 for individual farmers; up to ₹10 lakh for cooperatives
deadline: Applications typically open June–September each year
source: Haryana Agriculture Department — CRM Scheme Guidelines

name: Crop Residue Management (CRM) Scheme — Punjab
description: The Punjab Government, in collaboration with the Central Government under the CRM Scheme, provides subsidies on in-situ crop residue management machinery. Individual farmers receive 50% subsidy (up to ₹1.5 lakh) and Custom Hiring Centres (CHCs), cooperatives, and FPOs receive 80% subsidy on machinery like Happy Seeder, Super SMS, Zero Till Drill, Mulcher, etc.
eligibility: All farmers of Punjab with valid land records
Custom Hiring Centres (CHCs) established by cooperative societies
Priority to farmers in high stubble-burning districts
howToApply: Register at agrimachinerypb.com or Punjab Agri portal
Login and apply under the CRM scheme section
Select the machinery category and upload documents
Application verified at block level by Agriculture Development Officer (ADO)
After approval, purchase from empanelled manufacturer
Subsidy credited directly to bank account after verification
maxAmount: ₹1,50,000 for individual farmers; up to ₹12 lakh for CHCs/cooperatives
deadline: Rolling enrollments, typically peak in July–October before Kharif harvest

name: Central Sector Scheme on CRM — Government of India
description: The Central Government's CRM Scheme, implemented through the Ministry of Agriculture, promotes in-situ management of crop residue in Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, and NCR states. The scheme funds establishment of Custom Hiring Centres, farm machinery banks, and provides subsidies on individual machinery purchases to prevent stubble burning.
eligibility: Farmers in Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, and NCR Delhi
Individual farmers with verified land records
documentsRequired: Land documents as per state requirements
howToApply: Apply through respective state agriculture department portal
State government aggregates applications under central scheme
Block-level officer verifies eligibility
Central funds released to state, state disburses to farmer
Physical verification of machinery post-purchase
maxAmount: As per state implementation guidelines — typically ₹1.5 lakh for individuals
deadline: Aligned with state-level deadlines — generally pre-Kharif season
source: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare — CRM Scheme Guidelines

name: Crop Residue Management Scheme — Uttar Pradesh
description: Uttar Pradesh provides subsidies under the central CRM scheme for purchase of straw management machinery. The state focuses on western UP districts adjacent to NCR where paddy stubble burning contributes to winter air pollution. Individual farmers get 50% subsidy and groups/CHCs get 80% subsidy.
eligibility: Farmers in notified districts of western Uttar Pradesh
Custom Hiring Centres established by cooperatives/FPOs
Priority for small and marginal farmers
howToApply: Visit the UP Agriculture Department portal (upagriculture.com)
Register using Aadhaar number
Apply under CRM/Stubble Management section
Upload required documents
Block-level Agriculture Officer conducts verification
On approval, purchase machinery from approved dealer
Subsidy released to bank account after verification
